content_en:
“Fear the du‘ a of he who has been wronged, even if he is a

disbeliever, for there remains no veil between it”.

This hadith means that there is no veil between it and Allah, and Allah responds to the du’ a@ of the person who has been wronged.

262 Narrated by al-Hakim from Ibn ‘Umar, and authenticated in Sahih al-

263 Narrated by Ahmad and Abo Ya'la, and authentiated in Sahih al-Jami # 119.

This hadith also shows that there is no difference between doing wrong to a Muslim or to a disbeliever, and that both of their du‘ as are answered by Allah. If this is the case of the disbeliever, then how much more so the sinful Muslim! In fact, the Prophet (3%) said:

The dud of one who has been wronged is responded to, even

if he is a fastg (evil person), for his evil will only be against himself.’

So even a sinful Muslim will be responded to, for his sin will be against him, and will not prevent his dua from being answered.

In another narration we are told,

Three people’s du’ as are never rejected: the one who is fasting, until he breaks his fast; the just ruler; and the one who has been wronged. Allah raises it above the clouds, and the doors of the skies are opened for it, and the Lord says, ‘By My Hon-

our and Glory! I will help you, even if it be after some time!”

This is a very severe warning to the one who wrongs others, and a comfort and solace to one who has been wronged. Allah has sworn that He will, of a surety, come to the aid of the one who has been wronged.

It is appropriate here to quote the story of the noble Companion, Sa‘d ibn Abi Waqas, when ‘Umar appointed him governor over Kufah. Some people of Kifah complained to ‘Umar about Sa‘d, so ‘Umar sent forth his servants to go around the masids of Kufah, | asking the people’s opinion about Sa'd. Wherever the messengers went, they could only find people praising Sa‘d, except in one masjid, where a man by the name of Aba Sa‘d stood up and said: “If you

264 Reported by al-Tayalisi in his Musnad (# 1266). See al-Sahihah, # 767.

265 Reported by al-Tirmidhi (# 3598), who declared it to be hasan. However, al-Albani pointed out that it has a very slight weakness in it in a/-Da ‘ifah, # 1358. Although it is weak with this wording, the phrase pertinent to the

response of the du’a is definetely authentic, as al-Albani himself points out in al-Sahihah, # 870.

are really asking us by Allah, then know that he was not just in his judgements, nor did he distribute the booty equally, nor was he easy with us”.

At that, Sa‘d stood up and said: “O Allah! Ifhe is lying, then take away his sight, and give him a long life, and make trials afflict him!”

The narrator of this event states: “I saw him after a long time, blind, his eyelids were drooping (out of old age), and he used to harass the little girls as they walked in the alleys. Whenever he was asked, ‘How are you?’ he would respond, ‘I am an old man, great

trials have befallen me! The du‘a of Sa'd has been inflicted upon me .

In another incident, a woman accused Sa ‘id ibn Zayd, one of the Prophet’s (#%) fomous Companions of stealing some of her property. They appeared before the ruler, and Sa‘id prayed, “O Allah! If you know her to be lying, then make her blind, and make her grave in her own house”. The narrator of this incident said, “I saw her (later on), blind. She used to walk touching the walls, and say, ‘The dua of Sa‘id has afflicted me!’ Once, she passed by a well inside her house, and fell into it, so it became her grave”. ”

In these narrations we see the great miracle that Allah blessed both Sa‘d and Sa‘id with, and we also see the dangers of abusing others. Is there not a lesson in this story for those that wrongly accuse other Muslims of matters that they are free of? And how many situations have occurred, where the one that has been wronged has raised his hands to Allah, and seen with his own eyes the response of his du‘@. So beware, O Muslim, of oppressing other people in any form, and take comfort and solace, O you who have been wronged, that Allah will indeed respond to your du’ a.

266 Reported by al-Bukhari (# 755), Ahmad (1/175) and others. *67 Reported by Muslim (# 1610), and others.

2. The One in Severe Circumstances

When a person finds himself in a grave crisis, and his heart is about to shatter with grief and fear, at this point, he turns to Allah with a heart like that of no other person. The quality of sincerity that he displays, and the desperate need that he feels for the Mercy of his Lord, is so strong and pure that it is a du‘ a that is answered in all situations, regardless even of whether the person is a Muslim or not! Yes, even the du‘ a of the kafir is sometimes accepted, and that is part of the perfection of Allah’s Lordship, for He is the Rabb of the Muslim and the aftr.

Allah describes Himself as:

Nest heh At al

Is not He (the One) who responds to the distressed one when he calls out to Him?”

So if Allah responds even to the disbeliever in this situation, then how about the sinful Muslim? And how about the sincere, pious believer, how can he give up hope of being answered?

Al-Qurtubi wrote: “Allah has guaranteed the response of the du‘ @ of the one in distress, as He Himself informed us of this (guarantee). And the reason for this is that the one in distress will turn to Him with a pure sincerity, cutting his hope off from all other sources. And sincerity to Him is the cause of (gaining) His protection, regardless of whether it emanates from a kafir or Muslim, evil or pious!”

3. After a Calamity

One of the occasions in which the du‘ a of a person is answered is after a calamity has befallen him. However, it is necessary that the person display patience and satisfaction at Allah’s decree, and not wail or lament over his misfortune.

268 seerah al-Naml, 62. 269 41-Jami’ li Ahkam al-Qur’an, 13/223.

Umm Salamah narrated that the Prophet (3) said:

There is no Muslim that is afflicted with a calamity, and he says what Allah has commanded him to say: “To Allah we belong and to Him we will return! O Allah! Give me the rewards (of being patient over) this calamity, and grant me something better than itto replace it, except that Allah will give him something better to replace it.

Umm Salamah said, “So when (my husband) Aba Salamah died, I said this du‘ a, but could not help thinking, ‘Who is better than Abii Salamah (i.e. no one can replace Abi Salamah)?’ Then the Prophet (#%) himself sent me a messenger proposing to me, so Allah blessed me with someone better than Aba Salamah”.”

The person who has left his house, and is on a journey, is a stranger in the land that he travels to, alone and away from his family and friends. As such, Allah, all Glory and Praise be to Him, sends His Mercy upon a traveller, especially if he is travelling for the sake of knowledge or with the intention of performing any other act of worship, such as pilgrimage or jihad.

A traveller has been guaranteed that his du’ @ will be answered,

for the Prophet (#8) said:

“Three are the du’ as that are responded to, there is no doubt concerning them: the du‘ a of he who has been wronged, the

traveller, and the du’ a of the father for his son”!

If this is the case of the average traveller who is travelling for worldly reasons, then it applies even more when the person is travelling for religious reasons, such as desiring to increase his knowledge, or visit his parents, or visit a Muslim brother.

*Narrated by Muslim (# 918), Aba Dawad (# 3119), al-Tirmidhi (# 3511) and others. The addition of Umm Salamah’s story is found in some of the books of seerah.

27\Narrated by Abu Dawad (# 1535), al-Tirmidhi (# 1905), and Ahmad, and authenticated in Sahih al-Jami’ # 3031.

5. The Father Against his Son or for his Son

The proof for this condition has been mentioned in the previous

hadith. In another wording of the hadith, the Prophet (#8) said:

Three are the du'as that are responded to ... the dua of the

father against his son.

The dua of a father for his son could be a dua of blessings and mercy for him if the father is content and happy with his son.On the other hand, the father can make a dw‘d against his son, if the son does not treat his father properly. In this, there is a strong encouragement to Muslims to treat their parents with kindness, and to avoid causing distress to them in any way. This is also a warning to parents not to rush in making a du’ @ against their children, for they might make a dua in a state of anger which they would later regret.

The du‘a of the mother is also included in this hadith, for the right of the mother is even greater than that of the father. The dua of the parents for their daughters comes under this hadith as well, since the rulings applicable to men also apply to women unless there exists evidence to the contrary.

The story of Jurayj and his mother was mentioned earlier, and this story also proves this point. When Jurayj’s mother prayed against him because he did not obey her, Allah responded to her du'as.

6. The Son for the Father

While the father is alive, the son can benefit from him by pleasing him, thus ensuring that he makes du’a for his son. After the father dies, then the son must repay some of the sacrifices that the father made for him, and part of this can be done by sincerely praying for him after his death.

The Prophet (3%) said:

*?. Narrated by al-Bukhari in his Adab al-Mufrad (# 481) and authenticated in Sahih al-Adab (# 372).
content_id:
"Takutlah terhadap du'a orang yang dizalimi, meskipun ia seorang

kafir, karena tidak ada hijab (penghalang) antara du'a itu (dengan Allah)".

Hadith ini bermakna bahwa tidak ada hijab antara du'a tersebut dengan Allah, dan Allah mengabulkan du'a orang yang dizalimi.

262 Diriwayatkan oleh al-Hakim dari Ibn 'Umar, dan diautentikasi dalam Sahih al-

263 Diriwayatkan oleh Ahmad dan Abu Ya'la, dan diautentikasi dalam Sahih al-Jami' # 119.

Hadith ini juga menunjukkan bahwa tidak ada perbedaan antara berbuat zalim kepada seorang Muslim atau kepada seorang kafir, dan bahwa du'a keduanya dikabulkan oleh Allah. Jika demikian halnya dengan orang kafir, maka betapa lebih lagi dengan seorang Muslim yang berdosa! Bahkan, Nabi (صلى الله عليه وسلم) bersabda:

Du'a orang yang dizalimi dikabulkan, meskipun ia seorang fasiq (orang jahat), karena kejahatannya hanya akan menimpa dirinya sendiri.'

Maka bahkan seorang Muslim yang berdosa pun akan dikabulkan du'anya, karena dosanya akan ditanggung olehnya sendiri, dan tidak akan menghalangi du'anya dari dikabulkan.

Dalam riwayat lain disebutkan,

Du'a tiga orang tidak pernah ditolak: orang yang berpuasa, hingga ia berbuka; penguasa yang adil; dan orang yang dizalimi. Allah mengangkatnya melampaui awan, pintu-pintu langit dibukakan untuknya, dan Rabb berfirman, 'Demi Kehormatan dan Keagungan-Ku! Aku pasti akan menolongmu, meskipun setelah beberapa waktu!'

Ini adalah peringatan yang sangat keras bagi orang yang menzalimi orang lain, dan sebuah ketenangan serta hiburan bagi orang yang dizalimi. Allah telah bersumpah bahwa Dia pasti akan menolong orang yang dizalimi.

Tepat kiranya untuk dikutip di sini kisah sahabat mulia Sa'd ibn Abi Waqqas, ketika 'Umar mengangkatnya sebagai gubernur atas Kufah. Sebagian penduduk Kufah mengadukan Sa'd kepada 'Umar, maka 'Umar mengutus para pembantunya untuk berkeliling ke masjid-masjid Kufah, menanyakan pendapat masyarakat tentang Sa'd. Di mana pun para utusan itu pergi, mereka hanya menemukan orang-orang yang memuji Sa'd, kecuali di satu masjid, di mana seorang laki-laki bernama Abu Sa'd berdiri dan berkata: "Jika kalian

264 Dilaporkan oleh al-Thayalisi dalam Musnad-nya (# 1266). Lihat al-Sahihah, # 767.

265 Dilaporkan oleh al-Tirmidhi (# 3598), yang menyatakannya hasan. Namun, al-Albani menunjukkan bahwa terdapat kelemahan yang sangat ringan padanya dalam al-Dha'ifah, # 1358. Meskipun lemah dengan lafaz ini, ungkapan yang berkaitan dengan

pengabulan du'a jelas-jelas sahih, sebagaimana al-Albani sendiri tunjukkan dalam al-Sahihah, # 870.

benar-benar bertanya kepada kami demi Allah, maka ketahuilah bahwa ia tidak adil dalam keputusan-keputusannya, tidak membagi ghanimah secara merata, dan tidak mudah terhadap kami".

Mendengar itu, Sa'd berdiri dan berkata: "Ya Allah! Jika ia berdusta, maka cabutlah penglihatannya, panjangkan umurnya, dan timpakanlah cobaan kepadanya!"

Perawi peristiwa ini menyatakan: "Aku melihatnya setelah sekian lama, dalam keadaan buta, kelopak matanya terkulai (karena usia tua), dan ia sering mengganggu anak-anak perempuan kecil yang berjalan di gang-gang. Setiap kali ditanya, 'Bagaimana keadaanmu?' ia menjawab, 'Aku ini orang tua yang telah ditimpa cobaan besar! Du'a Sa'd telah menimpaku'."

Dalam peristiwa lain, seorang wanita menuduh Sa'id ibn Zayd, salah seorang sahabat Nabi (صلى الله عليه وسلم) yang terkenal, telah merampas sebagian hartanya. Mereka menghadap penguasa, dan Sa'id berdoa, "Ya Allah! Jika Engkau mengetahui bahwa ia berdusta, maka butakanlah matanya, dan jadikanlah kuburannya di dalam rumahnya sendiri". Perawi peristiwa ini berkata: "Aku melihatnya (kemudian), dalam keadaan buta. Ia berjalan sambil meraba-raba dinding, dan berkata, 'Du'a Sa'id telah menimpaku!' Suatu ketika, ia melewati sebuah sumur di dalam rumahnya, lalu terjatuh ke dalamnya, sehingga itulah yang menjadi kuburannya".

Dalam riwayat-riwayat ini kita menyaksikan mukjizat besar yang Allah anugerahkan kepada Sa'd dan Sa'id, dan kita juga melihat bahaya menyakiti orang lain. Tidakkah ada pelajaran dalam kisah ini bagi mereka yang dengan zalim menuduh sesama Muslim dengan hal-hal yang tidak mereka lakukan? Dan betapa banyak situasi telah terjadi, di mana orang yang dizalimi mengangkat tangannya kepada Allah, lalu menyaksikan sendiri pengabulan du'anya. Maka berhati-hatilah, wahai Muslim, dari menzalimi orang lain dalam bentuk apa pun, dan ambillah ketenangan serta hiburan, wahai orang yang dizalimi, bahwa Allah sungguh-sungguh akan mengabulkan du'amu.

266 Dilaporkan oleh al-Bukhari (# 755), Ahmad (1/175) dan lainnya. 267 Dilaporkan oleh Muslim (# 1610), dan lainnya.

2. Orang yang Berada dalam Keadaan Sangat Terdesak

Ketika seseorang mendapati dirinya dalam krisis yang parah, dan hatinya hampir hancur karena kesedihan dan ketakutan, pada saat itulah ia berpaling kepada Allah dengan hati yang tidak tertandingi oleh siapa pun. Kualitas ikhlas yang ia tunjukkan, dan kebutuhan mendesak yang ia rasakan akan Rahmat Rabbnya, begitu kuat dan murni sehingga du'a tersebut adalah du'a yang dikabulkan dalam segala keadaan, bahkan meskipun orangnya bukan Muslim! Ya, bahkan du'a orang kafir pun terkadang dikabulkan, dan itu adalah bagian dari kesempurnaan Rububiyyah Allah, karena Dia adalah Rabb orang Muslim maupun kafir.

Allah menggambarkan diri-Nya sebagai:

Nest heh At al

"Bukankah Dia (yang Maha Esa) yang mengabulkan (du'a) orang yang dalam keadaan terdesak ketika ia berdoa kepada-Nya?"

Jika Allah mengabulkan bahkan du'a orang kafir dalam situasi ini, lalu bagaimana dengan seorang Muslim yang berdosa? Dan bagaimana dengan mukmin yang tulus dan saleh, bagaimana mungkin ia berputus asa dari dikabulkan?

Al-Qurtubi menulis: "Allah telah menjamin pengabulan du'a orang yang dalam keadaan terdesak, sebagaimana Dia sendiri telah memberitahukan jaminan ini kepada kita. Dan alasannya adalah bahwa orang yang dalam keadaan terdesak akan berpaling kepada-Nya dengan ikhlas yang murni, memutuskan harapannya dari segala sumber lain. Dan ikhlas kepada-Nya adalah sebab (memperoleh) perlindungan-Nya, tidak peduli apakah itu berasal dari seorang kafir atau Muslim, orang jahat atau saleh!"

3. Setelah Tertimpa Musibah

Salah satu keadaan di mana du'a seseorang dikabulkan adalah setelah musibah menimpanya. Namun, diperlukan agar orang tersebut menampilkan kesabaran dan keridaan terhadap ketetapan Allah, dan tidak meratap atau meraung atas musibah yang menimpanya.

268 Lihat Surat al-Naml, 62. 269 Al-Jami' li Ahkam al-Qur'an, 13/223.

Umm Salamah meriwayatkan bahwa Nabi (صلى الله عليه وسلم) bersabda:

Tidak ada seorang Muslim yang ditimpa musibah, lalu ia mengucapkan apa yang Allah perintahkan untuk diucapkan: "Inna lillahi wa inna ilaihi raji'un! Ya Allah! Berikanlah kepadaku pahala (atas kesabaranku menghadapi) musibah ini, dan anugerahkanlah kepadaku sesuatu yang lebih baik sebagai gantinya," melainkan Allah akan memberikan kepadanya sesuatu yang lebih baik sebagai gantinya.

Umm Salamah berkata: "Maka ketika (suamiku) Abu Salamah wafat, aku mengucapkan du'a ini, namun tidak dapat menahan pikiran, 'Siapa yang lebih baik dari Abu Salamah (yakni tidak ada yang dapat menggantikan Abu Salamah)?' Kemudian Nabi (صلى الله عليه وسلم) sendiri mengutus utusan untuk melamarku, maka Allah menganugerahiku dengan seseorang yang lebih baik dari Abu Salamah"."

Orang yang telah meninggalkan rumahnya dan sedang dalam perjalanan adalah seorang asing di tanah yang ia tuju, sendirian dan jauh dari keluarga serta teman-temannya. Oleh karena itu, Allah, Mahamulia dan Mahaterpuji, menurunkan Rahmat-Nya kepada seorang musafir, terutama jika ia bepergian demi mencari ilmu atau dengan niat melaksanakan ibadah lainnya, seperti haji atau jihad.

Seorang musafir telah dijamin bahwa du'anya akan dikabulkan,

karena Nabi (صلى الله عليه وسلم) bersabda:

"Tiga du'a yang dikabulkan, tidak ada keraguan mengenainya: du'a orang yang dizalimi, du'a musafir, dan du'a seorang ayah untuk anaknya"!

Jika demikian halnya dengan musafir biasa yang bepergian karena urusan duniawi, maka hal ini lebih berlaku lagi ketika seseorang bepergian untuk tujuan keagamaan, seperti ingin menambah ilmu, atau mengunjungi orang tuanya, atau mengunjungi saudara sesama Muslim.

*Diriwayatkan oleh Muslim (# 918), Abu Dawud (# 3119), al-Tirmidhi (# 3511) dan lainnya. Tambahan kisah Umm Salamah terdapat dalam beberapa kitab sirah.

271 Diriwayatkan oleh Abu Dawud (# 1535), al-Tirmidhi (# 1905), dan Ahmad, dan diautentikasi dalam Sahih al-Jami' # 3031.

5. Du'a Ayah yang Ditujukan Kepada Anaknya atau untuk Anaknya

Dalil untuk kondisi ini telah disebutkan dalam hadith sebelumnya. Dalam lafaz lain dari hadith tersebut, Nabi (صلى الله عليه وسلم) bersabda:

Tiga du'a yang dikabulkan ... du'a seorang ayah yang ditujukan kepada anaknya.

Du'a seorang ayah untuk anaknya bisa berupa du'a keberkahan dan rahmat bagi sang anak jika ayah merasa puas dan bahagia dengan anaknya. Di sisi lain, ayah dapat membuat du'a yang ditujukan kepada anaknya, jika sang anak tidak memperlakukan ayahnya dengan baik. Dalam hal ini, terdapat dorongan kuat bagi umat Muslim untuk memperlakukan orang tua mereka dengan penuh kebaikan, dan menghindari menyebabkan kesusahan kepada mereka dalam bentuk apa pun. Ini juga merupakan peringatan bagi para orang tua untuk tidak terburu-buru membuat du'a yang ditujukan kepada anak-anak mereka, karena mereka mungkin membuat du'a dalam keadaan marah yang kemudian akan mereka sesali.

Du'a seorang ibu juga termasuk dalam hadith ini, karena hak seorang ibu bahkan lebih besar daripada hak seorang ayah. Du'a orang tua untuk anak-anak perempuan mereka juga tercakup dalam hadith ini, karena hukum yang berlaku bagi laki-laki juga berlaku bagi perempuan kecuali terdapat dalil yang menyatakan sebaliknya.

Kisah Jurayj dan ibunya telah disebutkan sebelumnya, dan kisah ini juga membuktikan hal tersebut. Ketika ibu Jurayj berdoa menentangnya karena ia tidak menaatinya, Allah mengabulkan du'a-nya.

6. Anak untuk Ayahnya

Selama ayah masih hidup, seorang anak dapat mengambil manfaat darinya dengan menyenangkan hatinya, sehingga memastikan bahwa sang ayah memanjatkan du'a untuk anaknya. Setelah sang ayah meninggal, maka sang anak harus membalas sebagian dari pengorbanan yang telah dilakukan ayahnya untuknya, dan sebagian dari hal ini dapat dilakukan dengan tulus berdoa untuknya setelah kematiannya.

Nabi (3%) bersabda:

*?. Diriwayatkan oleh al-Bukhari dalam Adab al-Mufrad-nya (# 481) dan diotentikasi dalam Sahih al-Adab (# 372).
content_ar: “الخوف the du‘ a of he who has been wronged, even إذا he is a disbeliever, for there remains no veil between it”. This الحديث means that there is no veil between it and الله, and الله responds to the du’ a@ of the person who has been wronged. 262 Narrated by al-Hakim from Ibn ‘عمر, and authenticated in صحيح al- 263 Narrated by أحمد and Abo Ya'la, and authentiated in صحيح al-Jami # 119. This الحديث أيضاً shows that there is no difference between doing الخطأ to a مسلم or to a disbeliever, and that both of their du‘ as are answered by الله. إذا this is the case of the disbeliever, then how much more لذا the sinful مسلم! In fact, النبي ﷺ (3%) said: The dud of one who has been wronged is responded to, even إذا he is a fastg (الشر person), for his الشر will only be against himself.’ لذا even a sinful مسلم will be responded to, for his الذنب will be against him, and will not prevent his الدعاء from being answered. In another narration we are told, Three people’s du’ as are never rejected: the one who is الصيام, حتى he breaks his fast; the just ruler; and the one who has been wronged. الله raises it above the clouds, and the doors of the skies are opened for it, and the Lord says, ‘By My Hon- our and Glory! I will help you, even إذا it be بعد some time!” This is a very severe warning to the one who wrongs others, and a comfort and solace to one who has been wronged. الله has sworn that He will, of a surety, come to the aid of the one who has been wronged. It is appropriate here to quote the story of the noble Companion, Sa‘d ibn Abi Waqas, عندما ‘عمر appointed him governor over Kufah. Some people of Kifah complained to ‘عمر about Sa‘d, لذا ‘عمر sent forth his servants to go around the masids of Kufah, | asking the people’s opinion about Sa'd. Wherever the الرسل went, they could only find people praising Sa‘d, except in one المسجد, where a man by the name of Aba Sa‘d stood up and said: “إذا you 264 Reported by al-Tayalisi in his Musnad (# 1266). See al-Sahihah, # 767. 265 Reported by al-الترمذي (# 3598), who declared it to be حسن. غير أن, al-Albani pointed out that it has a very slight الضعف in it in a/-Da ‘ifah, # 1358. وإن كان it is weak with this wording, the phrase pertinent to the response of the du’a is definetely authentic, as al-Albani himself points out in al-Sahihah, # 870. are really asking us by الله, then know that he was not just in his judgements, nor did he distribute the booty equally, nor was he easy with us”. At that, Sa‘d stood up and said: “O الله! Ifhe is lying, then take away his sight, and give him a long الحياة, and make الابتلاءات afflict him!” The narrator of this event states: “I saw him بعد a long time, blind, his eyelids were drooping (out of old age), and he used to harass the little girls as they walked in the alleys. كلما he was asked, ‘How are you?’ he would respond, ‘I am an old man, great الابتلاءات have befallen me! The du‘a of Sa'd has been inflicted upon me . In another incident, a woman accused Sa ‘id ibn Zayd, one of النبي ﷺ’s (#%) fomous الصحابة of stealing some of her property. They appeared قبل the ruler, and Sa‘id prayed, “O الله! إذا you know her to be lying, then make her blind, and make her القبر in her own house”. The narrator of this incident said, “I saw her (later on), blind. She used to walk touching the walls, and say, ‘The الدعاء of Sa‘id has afflicted me!’ حين, she passed by a well inside her house, and fell into it, لذا it became her القبر”. ” In these narrations we see the great miracle that الله blessed both Sa‘d and Sa‘id with, and we أيضاً see the dangers of abusing others. Is there not a lesson in this story for those that wrongly accuse other المسلمون of matters that they are free of? And how many situations have occurred, where the one that has been wronged has raised his hands to الله, and seen with his own eyes the response of his du‘@. لذا beware, O مسلم, of oppressing other people in any form, and take comfort and solace, O you who have been wronged, that الله will indeed respond to your du’ a. 266 Reported by البخاري (# 755), أحمد (1/175) and others. *67 Reported by مسلم (# 1610), and others. 2. The One in Severe Circumstances عندما a person finds himself in a القبر crisis, and his القلب is about to shatter with الحزن and الخوف, at this point, he turns to الله with a القلب like that of no other person. The quality of sincerity that he displays, and the desperate need that he feels for the الرحمة of his Lord, is لذا strong and pure that it is a du‘ a that is answered in all situations, regardless even of whether the person is a مسلم or not! Yes, even the du‘ a of the kafir is sometimes accepted, and that is part of the perfection of الله’s Lordship, for He is the Rabb of the مسلم and the aftr. الله describes Himself as: Nest heh At al Is not He (the One) who responds to the distressed one عندما he calls out to Him?” لذا إذا الله responds even to the disbeliever in this situation, then how about the sinful مسلم? And how about the sincere, pious المؤمن, how can he give up الأمل of being answered? Al-Qurtubi wrote: “الله has guaranteed the response of the du‘ @ of the one in distress, as He Himself informed us of this (guarantee). And the العقل for this is that the one in distress will turn to Him with a pure sincerity, cutting his الأمل off from all other sources. And sincerity to Him is the cause of (gaining) His protection, regardless of whether it emanates from a kafir or مسلم, الشر or pious!” 3. بعد a Calamity One of the occasions in which the du‘ a of a person is answered is بعد a calamity has befallen him. غير أن, من الضروري that the person display الصبر and satisfaction at الله’s decree, and not wail or lament over his misfortune. 268 seerah al-Naml, 62. 269 41-Jami’ li Ahkam al-Qur’an, 13/223. Umm Salamah narrated that النبي ﷺ (3) said: There is no مسلم that is afflicted with a calamity, and he says what الله has commanded him to say: “To الله we belong and to Him we will return! O الله! Give me the rewards (of being patient over) this calamity, and grant me something better than itto replace it, except that الله will give him something better to replace it. Umm Salamah said, “لذا عندما (my الزوج) Aba Salamah died, I said this du‘ a, but could not help thinking, ‘Who is better than Abii Salamah (i.e. no one can replace Abi Salamah)?’ Then النبي ﷺ (#%) himself sent me a messenger proposing to me, لذا الله blessed me with someone better than Aba Salamah”.” The person who has left his house, and is on a journey, is a stranger in the land that he travels to, alone and away from his الأسرة and friends. As such, الله, all Glory and Praise be to Him, sends His الرحمة upon a traveller, ولا سيما إذا he is travelling for the sake of العلم or with the intention of performing any other act of worship, مثل pilgrimage or jihad. A traveller has been guaranteed that his du’ @ will be answered, for النبي ﷺ (#8) said: “Three are the du’ as that are responded to, لا شك فيما يتعلق بـ them: the du‘ a of he who has been wronged, the traveller, and the du’ a of the الأب for his son”! إذا this is the case of the average traveller who is travelling for worldly reasons, then it applies even more عندما the person is travelling for religious reasons, مثل desiring to increase his العلم, or visit his الوالدان, or visit a مسلم الأخ. *Narrated by مسلم (# 918), Aba Dawad (# 3119), al-الترمذي (# 3511) and others. The addition of Umm Salamah’s story is found in some of the الكتب of seerah. 27\Narrated by Abu Dawad (# 1535), al-الترمذي (# 1905), and أحمد, and authenticated in صحيح al-Jami’ # 3031. 5. The الأب Against his Son or for his Son The proof for this condition has been mentioned in the previous الحديث. In another wording of the الحديث, النبي ﷺ (#8) said: Three are the du'as that are responded to ... the الدعاء of the الأب against his son. The الدعاء of a الأب for his son could be a الدعاء of البركات and الرحمة for him إذا the الأب is content and happy with his son.من ناحية أخرى, the الأب can make a dw‘d against his son, إذا the son does not treat his الأب properly. In this, there is a strong encouragement to المسلمون to treat their الوالدان with اللطف, and to avoid causing distress to them in any الطريق. This is أيضاً a warning to الوالدان not to rush in making a du’ @ against their الأولاد, for they might make a الدعاء in a state of الغضب which they would later regret. The du‘a of the الأم is أيضاً included in this الحديث, for the الحق of the الأم is even greater than that of the الأب. The الدعاء of the الوالدان for their daughters comes under this الحديث as well, بما أن the rulings applicable to men أيضاً apply to women إلا إذا there exists evidence to the contrary. The story of Jurayj and his الأم was mentioned earlier, and this story أيضاً proves this point. عندما Jurayj’s الأم prayed against him لأن he did not obey her, الله responded to her du'as. 6. The Son for the الأب بينما the الأب is alive, the son can benefit from him by pleasing him, وهكذا ensuring that he makes du’a for his son. بعد the الأب dies, then the son must repay some of the sacrifices that the الأب made for him, and part of this can be done by sincerely praying for him بعد his الموت. النبي ﷺ (3%) said: *?. Narrated by البخاري in his Adab al-Mufrad (# 481) and authenticated in صحيح al-Adab (# 372).
